Saturday Summary: Winnasedge continues Caulfield love affair with Sir John Monash triumph

Angels Fury confirmed her growing reputation with a commanding Caulfield victory on Saturday, while Proxima Dea, Apex Predator and Latoya also impressed, delivering a quartet of standout juvenile performances across Australia and New Zealand.

Blue Bolt lived up to her name, bolting in to score success in the G1 Falmouth Stakes at Newmarket, while the impressive 2-year-old Senorita Bonita became another Group winner for her in-form sire Starspangledbanner.

The Group 2-winning mare Miraval Rose looks to continue Grahame Begg's stable's outstanding form in the Sir John Monash Stakes, while other strong prospects step out at Caulfield and Randwick on a solid Saturday of July racing.

The Asian Racing Federation has reshaped Australia's black type landscape for the 2026-27 season, confirming a series of race upgrades and downgrades while reinforcing its authority over the country's pattern racing system until further notice.

Tengun Tommy's recent success has provided Queensland owner-breeder Paul Nugent with another milestone, as his first homebred continues to reward the patience and planning behind a growing racing and breeding operation that is building momentum.
An exciting new team-based Thoroughbred Racing League with venues including Santa Anita, Gulfstream Park, and Keeneland is being launched by Greg Maffei and Danny Epstien.

To transform a broodmare farm into a stallion property in a few short months has been the work of the Yulong team at Segenhoe Stud, led by stud manager Brian Clarke. With four stallions due to arrive in time for their stallion parade on August 22, the upgrades are part of an exciting new chapter for Segenhoe Stud. On Thursday, Yulong announced Danny Rolston would join their expanding team.

With three group winners this season, and running at a solid 60% runners to winners, Exceedance just needs that one headline horse to take him to the next level. The G1 Coolmore Stud Stakes winner added his seventh juvenile winner for the season when No Limits won on debut at Gosford on Thursday.
